sources.note: Official joint DOT/NYCT B44 SBS report used to disprove unrelated Nostrand projections.

Official DOT/MTA partnership release identifying the B46 SBS corridor.
Official Brooklyn Community Board 9 B49 resolution checked as later route-geography context only; it is not historical lane-onset proof.
Official B82 SBS board presentation covering the Kings Highway treatment.
Official Battery Place project flyer with physical extent and planned implementation.

Official NYC DOT action plan; its Battery Place project sheet explicitly lists X27 and X28 among routes served.
Official Church Avenue project description names B35, B103, BM3, and BM4.
Official NYC Open Data metadata for ycrg-ses3.
Authoritative NYC DOT Bus Lanes - Local Streets API snapshot; selected non-geometry rows are retained separately.
Official NYC DOT busway extents and launch month summaries.
NYC DOT official data catalog.
Official 2018 NYC DOT project index checked for the dated Fulton Street projection and linked public-meeting material.
Official project index exposing Church Avenue and Fresh Pond Road board material.
Official project index exposing Jay Street and Malcolm X Boulevard board material.
Official project index exposing Battery Place board material.
Official project index for 2023 bus-priority work.
Official project index exposing Flatbush/Utica/Avenue S board material.
Official project index exposing Flatbush and Livingston Street board material.
Official Brooklyn CB2 presentation for the Flatbush project.
Official retrospective identifies the bounded Livingston-to-State phase and B41/B67 route scope at season precision.
Official NYC DOT Flatbush announcement; establishes project and B41 context but not the registry's October 2 onset.
Official Brooklyn CB18 intersection presentation; it does not establish route-specific bus-lane onset.
Official community-advisory-board presentation for the Jay Street Busway.
Official Jay Street retrospective names seven routes and an August 2020 launch, not an exact launch day.
Official Livingston completion release names B41, B45, B67, and B103, none of the shard's Livingston candidates.
Official MTA Brooklyn bus map used only for route geography, never as historical implementation-date proof.
Official Third Avenue project source; it does not establish the candidate-specific December 2025 express-route phase.

web_search_method: Official-domain corridor searches were executed on 2026-07-15. Each receipt preserves the exact query and official URLs; retrieved official sources were byte-hashed.
